sutta: buddha said monks these 5 conditions lead to the decline of among in training what 5 delight in work or activity delight in gossip delight in sleeping delight in company and he does not reflect on the mind as free amongst these are the 5 conditions that is a very interesting suitable very important suta and where these 5 things can obstruct among from his cultivation the first 1 is having too much work or activity which means the monk is very much involved in teaching in writing books in administration supervising others in the construction of the temple supervising the construction of the temple planning etc and all this too much work can obstruct among spiritual cultivation [Music] second 1 is delight in gossip uh talking unbeneficial the buddha said now if we want to talk or especially monks and ip monks want to talk then we should engage in beneficial talk and a beneficial talk is talk concerned with wanting little few ones concerned with contentment seclusion aloofness from society arousing energy virtue concentration wisdom liberation knowledge and vision of liberation etc these are beneficial talk and what is uh useless talk is uh or unbeneficial talk is what is mentioned in the sutas estirachana tirachana literally means animal talk and it means a low talk based concern with politics sports entertainment traveling departed etc etc and that is not beneficial and the third 1 is delight in sleeping sleeping is 1 of the pleasures people get now if you sleep too much our mind every time we sleep we is our mind is like a computer every time we sleep we switch off many of the circuits in the mind and later to get into to activate them it's not so easy because normally in a normal day we don't use so many circuits in our computer up there so when we meditate a lot now we find that slowly the circuits in our brain start to activate that's why sometimes in meditation we get tingling sensation in the brain is like the circuit sound and normally not used in the brain start to activate so when we sleep too much now we shut off a lot of the circuits in our in our mind and it's not good 1 is delight in company that means you know the monk likes to keep in company talk with other people whether monks or lay people the opposite is aloofness that even though a monk stays in a monastery with other monks he tries to be aloof if it's necessary to talk he talks it is not necessary he does not talk the last 1 he does not reflect on the mind is free this is quite interesting very interesting statement because it reminds us something like mentioned in the yoga teachings that basically on zen teachings basically our mind is freedom it's the only the active mind the active mind the active consciousness the sex consciousness which makes us see ourselves as a human being in this world of samsara and there's a part of us that is immortal there's a part of us that is free and it's there all the time the only thing we have to realize it [Music] so that's the end of the suta
